description: SAVAK (Sāzemān-e Ettelā'āt va Amniyat-e Keshvar — Organization of Intelligence and National Security) was the secret police, domestic security, and intelligence service of Iran under [Mohammad Reza Shah Pahlavi](/people/mohammad-reza-shah-pahlavi). Established in 1957 with CIA and Mossad assistance, it suppressed political opposition including the [National Front](/orgs/national-front-iran), the [Freedom Movement of Iran](/orgs/freedom-movement-of-iran), leftist organizations, and Islamist groups. Known for systematic use of torture, it imprisoned thousands of political dissidents. Dissolved after the [1979 revolution](/events/iranian-revolution-1979).
editorial_notes: Founded 1957 with CIA/Mossad help. Dissolved Feb 1979. Replaced by SAVAMA then MOIS.
